divergences: Desktop wallpaper (macOS 'Flow'-style purple/blue/magenta/orange swirl with a diagonal silver light ribbon) is approximated with sampled CSS linear-gradient bands per edge, not a procedural/photographic reproduction; a true rotated-diagonal ribbon overlay was tried and measured worse than the per-edge vertical/horizontal gradient approximation, so the simpler approximation was kept.

Window vibrancy material is painted as a hand-tuned opaque gradient (radial warm-purple/magenta blobs over a vertical navy base) calibrated against sampled interior colors (header/composer/bubble/thinking/markdown/footer regions), rather than relying on backdrop-filter blur of the synthetic wallpaper underneath — real backdrop-filter blur of our approximated wallpaper produced unrealistic sharp-edge bleed (visible orange/gray patches) that a real macOS vibrancy blur would not show over the true wallpaper.

Remaining error is dominated by text glyph edge antialiasing differences between headless-Chromium rendering and the real macOS Retina screenshot: word-level crops (e.g. the user bubble's first line, the 'Tip:' line, the footer labels) are visually pixel-identical in content, kerning, weight and position, yet contribute the largest share of per-pixel RMSE purely from AA/subpixel edge variance (measured: text-classified pixels ~6% of window area but ~60% of window MSE). Tried an alternate -webkit-font-smoothing value; it measured worse and was reverted.

Photographic fine detail in the reference (noise/speckle in the wallpaper corners, e.g. top-right and bottom-left) cannot be reproduced by flat CSS gradients and is visible as residual speckle in the diff image; this is treated as acceptable per the brief's photographic-detail allowance.
